# Pre-Lecture Videos in COMP110

<img src="{{ site.baseurl }}/static/imgs/logo.png" alt="Image of Comp110 rainbow logo." width="150"/>

## Project Overview

This project analyzes whether introducing pre lecture videos would create value to comp 110. The goal is to understand how different factors like how difficult a student percieved the class, if the student had prior programming experience, and if the student like lesson videos would effect if a student valued introducing pre lecture videos.

## Summary of Analysis

The dataset combines survey responses Alyssa and Izzi responses on these variables:
- Pre-lecture video preference
- Perceived course difficulty
- Understanding of material
- Lesson video effectiveness
- Prior programming experience

## Key Visualizations

### 1. Distribution of Pre-Lecture Video Preference
<img src="{{ site.baseurl }}/static/imgs/plv_distribution.png" width="600"/>

This chart shows that most students lean toward agreeing with the idea of pre lecture videos.

---

### 2. Difficulty vs Pre-Lecture Video Preference
<img src="{{ site.baseurl }}/static/imgs/difficulty_vs_plv.png" width="600"/>

Students who perceive the course as more difficult tend to prefer pre lecture videos more, suggesting these videos could help students that are struggling in the course.

---

### 3. Prior Experience vs Pre-Lecture Video Preference
<img src="{{ site.baseurl }}/static/imgs/prior_exp_vs_plv.png" width="600"/>

Students with less prior programming experience show a much higher intrestest in pre lecture videos. this suggests that it would be really benefit for new programmers.

---

### 4. Lesson Effectiveness vs Pre-Lecture Video Preference
<img src="{{ site.baseurl }}/static/imgs/lesson_vs_plv.png" width="600"/>

Students who already find lesson videos effective are also more likely to support pre lecture videos. This shows thats if you find video learning helpful you will likely support pre lecture videos too.

---

## Conclusion

Overall, the data suggests that students overall support pre-lecture videos, however the level of support is different between different groups. In particular, students with less prior programming experience, those who perceive the course as more difficult, and those who find lesson videos helpful tend to show stronger support for pre lecture videos. This shows that pre lecture videos could provide meaningful support for students who need need helpe, specifically targeting new programmers that are stuggling in comp110.
